session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 8544 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the skill and execute it with the specified arguments.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll execute the skill by running the `erk exec get-pr-feedback` command with the specified PR number:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Show file restructuring context for current branch

  "success": true,

    {
      "id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5xbEPL",

        {
          "author": "github-actions",
          "body": "**Audit PR Docs**: Drift risk — documents specific values/paths that will change.

Source: `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/rebase_cmd.py:28-30`

Risk: This section restates behavior already described in the `rebase()` docstring, including the specific `--dangerous` flag name. If the flag is renamed or the mid-rebase behavior changes, this prose will diverge from the docstring silently. The docstring at `rebase_cmd.py:28-30` is the canonical description of this behavior. Consider replacing with a source pointer:

```markdown
<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/pr/rebase_cmd.py, rebase -->
See the `rebase` command in `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/rebase_cmd.py` for full details on mid-rebase recovery.
```

Or keep the section but remove the `--dangerous` flag name, since that's an implementation detail that could change.",
          "created_at": "2026-03-01T19:00:59Z"
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "discussion_comments": [
    {
      "id": 3980751343,
      "author": "github-actions[bot]",
      "body": "<!-- erk:pr-address-run -->
## PR Review Comments Addressed

This PR was reviewed by an automated process triggered by:
```
erk pr address-remote 8544
```

### Result

No changes were needed to address the review comments.

This could mean:
- Comments were already addressed in a previous commit
- Comments were informational (no action required)
- Claude determined the existing code already satisfies the feedback

### Details

- **Model:** claude-opus-4-6
- **Workflow run:** [View details](https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/actions/runs/22550749324)

---
*Automated by erk pr-address workflow*",
      "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8544#issuecomment-3980751343"

      "body": "<!-- dignified-code-simplifier-review -->

## ✅ Dignified Code Simplifier Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-01 11:02:32 PT

No violations detected. All code changes comply with dignified-python standards.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ LBYL exception handling - None found
✅ Pathlib operations - None found  
✅ Import organization - None found
✅ Property/magic method performance - None found
✅ Indentation depth - None found
✅ Docstring clarity - Excellent

### Summary
This PR contains documentation updates and docstring enhancements. The Python code changes are limited to docstring additions in `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/rebase_cmd.py` (lines 28-30 and 43-46). These additions:
- Clearly explain the mid-rebase recovery capability
- Provide concrete examples of usage
- Are well-formatted and idiomatic
- Introduce no violations of dignified-python standards

The documentation updates in the learned docs and command descriptions are well-structured and maintain consistency with existing patterns.

### Files Reviewed
- `.claude/commands/erk/rebase.md`: Documentation only
- `docs/learned/architecture/index.md`: Documentation only
- `docs/learned/architecture/rebase-conflict-patterns.md`: Documentation only
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/rebase_cmd.py`: Docstring additions only (no violations)

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-01 11:02:32 PT**: No violations detected - docstring additions are clear and provide concrete examples
- **2026-03-01 11:01:22 PT**: No violations detected - docstring additions are clear and well-formatted
- **2026-03-01 11:00:30 PT**: No violations detected - docstring additions are clear and well-formatted

</details>",
      "body": "<!-- dignified-python-review -->

## ✅ Dignified Python Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-01 11:02:29 PT

No violations found. All Python code changes adhere to dignified-python standards.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ LBYL (no try/except for control flow) - None found
✅ Path operations (.exists() before .resolve()) - None found
✅ Exception handling at CLI boundary - Appropriate
✅ Import organization - None found
✅ Function signatures (keyword-only args) - Compliant
✅ Subprocess operations - None found
✅ CLI patterns (click.echo, no print) - Compliant
✅ Re-exports / __all__ - None found

### Files Reviewed
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/rebase_cmd.py`: 0 violations (docstring enhancements only)
- `.claude/commands/erk/rebase.md`: Documentation (no Python code)
- `docs/learned/architecture/rebase-conflict-patterns.md`: Documentation (no Python code)

### Findings
- Function signature at line 22 properly uses keyword-only args marker (`*`)
- LBYL guard check at lines 54-60 correctly checks `dangerous` flag before proceeding
- CLI error handling at lines 82-85 appropriate at command boundary
- All docstring additions provide clarity without introducing code violations

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-01 11:02:29 PT**: Re-verified - docstring updates to rebase command all compliant
- **2026-03-01 11:01:18 PT**: Complete re-review - verified docstring enhancements compliant
- **2026-03-01 11:01:01 PT**: Re-reviewed docstring updates - all compliant with dignified-python standards

</details>",

Replace --dangerous flag name with source pointer or remove implementation detail.", "complexity": "single_file", "original_comment": "**Audit PR Docs**: Drift risk — documents specific values/paths that will change.\n\nSource: `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/rebase_cmd.py:28-30`\n\nRisk: This section restates behavior already described in the `rebase()` docstring, including the specific `--dangerous` flag name." } ], "discussion_actions": [], "informational_count": 6, "batches": [ { "name": "Pre-Existing (Auto-Resolve)", "complexity": "pre_existing", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Local Fixes", "complexity": "local", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Single-File", "complexity": "single_file", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [0] }, { "name": "Cross-Cutting", "complexity": "cross_cutting", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Complex", "complexity": "complex", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Informational", "complexity": "informational", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] } ], "error": null } ```
**Summary:**
PR 8544 has **1 actionable review thread** and **6 informational discussion comments**:
- **Actionable thread (single_file)**: The bot-generated audit comment at line 55 flags a documentation drift risk where the new "Mid-Rebase Recovery" section restates the `--dangerous` flag name and behavior that mirrors the CLI docstring in `rebase_cmd.py`. The recommendation is to either replace with a source pointer or remove the implementation-specific flag name to prevent future divergence.
- **Informational comments** (6 total): These are auto-generated status updates from CI workflows (dignified-code-simplifier, dignified-python, audit-pr-docs, test-coverage-review, tripwires-review) and team workflow markers (starting implementation, Graphite stack comment). These don't require action changes but provide context on the PR's review status.
